records.ety: [OE. savour, savor, savur, OF. savor, savour, F. saveur, fr. L. sapor, fr. sapere to taste, savor. See Sage, a., and cf. Sapid, Insipid, Sapor.]

defs: That property of a thing which affects the organs of taste or smell; taste and odor; flavor; relish; scent; as, the savor of an orange or a rose; an ill savor.
quotes: I smell sweet savors and I feel soft things.

defs: Hence, specific flavor or quality; characteristic property; distinctive temper, tinge, taint, and the like.
quotes: Why is not my life a continual joy, and the savor of heaven perpetually upon my spirit?

defs: Sense of smell; power to scent, or trace by scent.

quotes: She shall no savor have therein but lite.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. savorer, F. savourer. See Savor, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Savored (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Savoring.]

defs: To have a particular smell or taste; -- with of.
To partake of the quality or nature; to indicate the presence or influence; to smack; -- with of.
quotes: This savors not much of distraction.
I have rejected everything that savors of party.

defs: To use the sense of taste.
quotes: By sight, hearing, smelling, tasting or savoring, and feeling.

defs: To perceive by the smell or the taste; hence, to perceive; to note.
To have the flavor or quality of; to indicate the presence of.
quotes: That cuts us off from hope, and savors only Rancor and pride, impatience and despite.

defs: To taste or smell with pleasure; to delight in; to relish; to like; to favor.
